bundles.changelog: First concrete foundation-bundle prerelease package (six foundation-document templates with their own schema and hash baselines).
Reworks the technical-architecture document so cross-cutting content lives in one place and is cross-referenced rather than duplicated.
Your built system can now APPLY foundation-document upgrades (not just detect them); upgrades became repeatable, quieter, and clearer, and merge your edits with the new version where possible.
You now build and run your system one capability at a time, accepting each before building the next (the build-and-operate loop with per-phase acceptance).

Your system now keeps you oriented and protects risky actions; can receive operating-layer improvements through an upgrade (not just foundation documents); and can check for and safely apply its own updates.
The approval drill (the one-time safety demonstration) now uses an example grounded in what your system actually does, instead of a generic action.
